subject: Correction: actor self-certified UAT with a non-independent verifier; false PASS repeated twice

claims.text: incident.txt states the actor and verifier shared context and that a false PASS was repeated twice.
claims.disconfirming_observation: incident.txt would describe the verifier as independent of the actor, or report at most a single PASS.

claims.text: A verifier sharing the actor's context is not independent, so the recorded PASS is a self-certification rather than an independent verification.
claims.disconfirming_observation: Evidence that the verifier reached the PASS through an oracle isolated from the actor's context.

claims.text: The 'PASS' functioned as a success proxy substituting for the authorized priority, which is an independently verified UAT (C2).
The claim 'UAT passed' is not backed by any independent authorization; a self-certification by an actor sharing the verifier's context never authorizes (C1).
claims.disconfirming_observation: An authorization record from an actor-independent verifier attesting the PASS.

claims.text: The earliest interruptible link in the recurring chain (shared context -> non-independent verification -> accepted self-certified PASS -> false PASS -> repeated) is the assignment of a verifier who shares the actor's context; assigning an actor-independent verifier before the next UAT breaks the recurrence (C4).
claims.disconfirming_observation: A subsequent false PASS occurring even after an independent verifier is assigned, indicating the defect lies elsewhere in the chain.

control_reason: The load-bearing PASS claim is a self-certification from a verifier sharing the actor's context (c2, c4) and the false PASS has already recurred twice (c1), so the verification oracle is inadequate and no independent authorization exists. Per C5/FAIL-CLOSED I reject the PASS and escalate for structural repair (verifier independence) rather than accept narrative closure; no action asserting the UAT passed is taken.
action: Record the correction: the self-certified UAT PASS is not accepted as verification. Do not certify or advance the work on the strength of this PASS. Escalate to require an actor-independent verifier (no shared context) before any future UAT PASS is accepted, targeting the earliest interruptible link (verifier assignment, c5).
residual_uncertainty: The exact mechanism of the shared context (same session, same memory, same agent) is not specified in incident.txt.
Whether an independent-verifier structure is available/enforceable is not stated in the artifact.
The subject under UAT and the substance of what was falsely PASSed are not described.
